generated_cells.notes: Setup: guarded imports, WIDGETS_AVAILABLE flag, seeded rng, style_axes helper, version/seed print, self-checks.
Markdown: hypothesis set H, linear regression as simplest model, running example.
Synthetic linear data from shared rng; scatter + one candidate line; shape and sorted asserts.
Markdown: MSE loss as aggregated distance, key properties.
loss(w,b) float-cast; interactive plot_line with fixed y-limits; FloatSlider interact; static fallback; truth-beats-zero assert.
Markdown: argmin, gradient descent rule, closed-form MSE gradients, learning rate.
gradient_descent with analytic gradients, initial point, non-finite early break, float arrays; example run + truth comparison + convergence asserts.
Precomputed loss surface ZZ; show_descent 1x2 contour+path+truth and loss-vs-iter (semilogy guard); diverged labeling; FloatLogSlider+IntSlider interact; static fallback.
Markdown: generalization, overfitting, train/test split motivation.
g(x)=sin(1.5x), M=30; shuffled 60/40 split re-sorted by x; disjointness assert; train/test scatter + true curve.
mse helper; plot_poly with RankWarning suppression (NumPy 1.x/2.x), degree cap, bounded y-limits, train/test MSE title; IntSlider interact; static fallback; monotone-train assert.

ridge_fit via np.vander with unpenalized intercept and lstsq fallback; DEG=12; plot_ridge bounded view, lambda=10**log_lambda; reuses mse; FloatSlider interact; static fallback; coefficient-shrink assert.
Markdown recap of three-step framework, demo summaries, extension suggestions.

dependency_notes: C04 creates x_train, y_train, true_w, true_b, N (shared rng from C02); reused unchanged by C06, C08, C09.
C06 defines loss(w,b); reused by C08 (printout/validation) and C09 (surface + curve).
C08 defines gradient_descent; reused by C09.
C11 creates x_train2, y_train2, x_test2, y_test2 and _pgrid; reused by C12 and C13.
C12 defines mse helper and _ylim2; both reused by C13.
WIDGETS_AVAILABLE from C02 gates every interact() call (C06, C09, C12, C13), each with a static fallback.
Every code cell references only earlier cells; no forward references.
assumptions: numpy, matplotlib are importable; ipywidgets is optional (guarded import with static fallbacks so the notebook runs without it).
np.random.default_rng is available (NumPy >= 1.17); a single seeded rng (SEED=42) is the sole randomness source for reproducibility.
RankWarning is resolved from np.RankWarning (NumPy 1.x) or np.exceptions.RankWarning (NumPy 2.x) so the polynomial demo suppresses fit warnings on either version.
Polynomial and ridge fits use closed-form least squares (np.polyfit / np.vander), not gradient descent, for speed and numerical stability.
true_w=2.0, true_b=-1.0 and g(x)=sin(1.5x) are illustrative constants chosen to satisfy the stage-3 test checks.
